products.title: Gold Nugget Maroon Clownfish Proaquatix (Friday Delivery)
products.body_html:
Premnas biaculeatus - The Gold Nugget Maroon Clownfish is a stunning designer variety of the Maroon Clownfish, prized for its rich maroon coloration accented by brilliant golden patterning. Each fish develops its own unique markings as it matures, making every individual a one of a kind addition to a marine aquarium. Juveniles have white patterns which darken to gold with age. Their bold personality, striking appearance, and hardy nature have made them a favorite among clownfish enthusiasts.

These captive bred clownfish are produced by our partners at Proaquatix and are raised entirely in aquaculture facilities. Being born and raised in captivity allows them to adapt readily to aquarium life and prepared foods, making them an excellent choice for both experienced reef keepers and those looking to keep a larger clownfish species for the first time.

Gold Nugget Maroon Clownfish readily accept a wide variety of prepared foods including high quality pellets, frozen foods, and other meaty marine diets. A varied diet rich in marine proteins will help maintain their vibrant coloration and overall health.

Like other Maroon Clownfish, this species can become territorial as it matures, particularly females. They are best kept singly, as a pair, or with tank mates that are able to coexist with their territorial personality. If housing with an anemone, they will readily host in suitable specie [Bubble tip anemones are a favorite, natural host), though an anemone is not required for them to thrive. 

If you want a pair, choose two juveniles at checkout, and they will form a pair with maturity.

Please choose a Friday delivery

products.title: John Deere Psammocora
products.body_html:
This Psammocora has been growing in our Biota Florida facility, and we're now offering in-house grown frags. 

Psammocora are small polyp stony corals, but they are hardier and more tolerant than other SPS corals. Provide moderate to high water movement and stable alkalinity, calcium, magnesium and trace elements. This species thrives under a variety of lighting conditions, but take care to slowly introduce it to higher lighting using your light's acclimation mode, or start the coral lower in the tank and slowly move it closer to the light over time.

products.title: Watermelon Rose Bubble Tip Anemone
products.body_html:
Entacmea quadricolor -  The Watermelon Rose Bubble Tip Anemone is a stunning color morph of the Bubble Tip Anemone, prized for its vibrant blend of rich rose red coloration accented by bright green highlights that resemble the rind of a watermelon. Under reef lighting, its fluorescent colors become even more striking, making it an eye catching centerpiece in any reef aquarium.

Like other Bubble Tip Anemones, this variety naturally seeks out a secure crevice in the rockwork where it can firmly attach its foot while extending its colorful tentacles into the current. Once settled, it is generally hardy and can thrive for many years in a mature, stable reef aquarium.

The Watermelon Rose Bubble Tip Anemone forms a fascinating symbiotic relationship with some clownfish, providing them with shelter while benefiting from the nutrients they contribute. While many clownfish readily accept Bubble Tip Anemones as hosts, the natural partner for this species are Maroon Clownfish.

This anemone is photosynthetic, but occasional feedings of small pieces of marine based meaty foods such as mysis shrimp, finely chopped seafood, or high quality frozen foods can promote healthy growth and encourage splitting in well established systems.

Care Requirements

  • Scientific Name: Entacmaea quadricolor
  • Difficulty: Moderate
  • Temperament: Semi-aggressive (stinging)
  • Lighting: Moderate to High
  • Flow: Moderate, indirect flow
  • Diet: Photosynthesis supplemented with occasional meaty foods
  • Reef Safe: Yes, with caution
  • Minimum Aquarium Size: 30 gallons

Notes

  • Allow the anemone to choose its own location after introduction. Moving it repeatedly can increase stress.
  • Provide adequate space from neighboring corals, as Bubble Tip Anemones possess a potent sting and may move until they find a preferred location.
  • A mature, stable aquarium with consistent salinity, alkalinity, and temperature is strongly recommended before adding any anemone.

products.title: Gold NPS Polyps
products.body_html:
Parazoanthus swiftii - Gold Polyps are a beautiful and fascinating Caribbean colonial zoantharian. Unlike many Pacific zoanthids, Parazoanthus swiftii naturally grows on a host sponge throughout the Caribbean. In aquariums, they don't require a host and appreciate moderate to strong water movement, which helps encourage full polyp extension. This non-photosynthetic species requires regular feedings of coral foods like Easy Reefs Easy SPS and Easy Booster.

These cultured specimens are produced by our partners at Bocas Mariculture in Panama, providing a sustainable alternative to wild collection.

products.title: Barsnout Cleaner Goby
products.body_html:
Elacatinus illecebrosus - The Barsnout Cleaner Goby is a rare and striking Caribbean cleaner goby known for its bold pattern and active personality. Like other members of the genus Elacatinus, Barsnout Gobies establish cleaning stations on the reef where larger fish visit to have parasites and dead tissue removed.

Despite their small size, these gobies are highly visible in the aquarium and spend much of their day perched in the open, interacting with tankmates and darting out to feed. Their peaceful temperament makes them excellent additions to nano reefs, Caribbean biotope aquariums, and larger community reef systems.

Captive bred Barsnout Gobies readily accept prepared foods, including pellets, frozen foods, and other aquarium diets. Aquacultured specimens are hardy, aquarium adapted, and help reduce collection pressure on wild reef populations.

These Barsnout Gobies are produced through sustainable aquaculture at Bocas Mariculture.

products.title: Yellowline Sponge Goby
products.body_html:
Elacatinus horsti - The Yellowline Goby is a small Caribbean goby prized for its bright yellow stripe, and peaceful disposition.

Elacatinus horsti is also frequently associated with tube sponges, using them as shelter while darting out to feed, Yellowline Gobies readily adapt to a variety of habitats and often become highly visible, perching in the open and interacting with tankmates.

Captive-bred Yellowline Gobies readily accept prepared foods, including pellets, frozen foods, and other aquarium diets. Their small size, peaceful nature, cleaning behavior, and hardiness make them an excellent choice for nano reefs and larger community aquariums alike.

Like all Biota fish, these Yellowline Gobies are aquacultured, helping reduce collection pressure on wild reefs while providing healthy, aquarium-adapted livestock.

products.title: Arrow Crab
products.body_html:
Stenorhychus seticornis  -  The Arrow Crab is one of the most unique invertebrates available for marine aquariums. With its impossibly long legs, triangular body, and curious personality, this Caribbean species is sure to draw attention from visitors and fellow hobbyists alike.

These cultured Arrow Crabs are produced by our partners at Bocas Mariculture in Panama, providing a sustainable alternative to wild collection. Arrow Crabs spend much of their time exploring rockwork in search of food and can help consume leftover meaty foods, detritus, and some nuisance bristleworms.

While generally reef compatible, Arrow Crabs are opportunistic scavengers and may prey on very small ornamental crustaceans or other sessile invertebrates if underfed. They are best suited for established aquariums with plenty of rockwork to explore and hide among.

Like all cultured livestock offered by Biota, these Arrow Crabs are part of our commitment to supporting sustainable marine aquaculture and reducing pressure on wild reef ecosystems.

products.title: Lemonpeel Angelfish Proaquatix (Friday Delivery)
products.body_html:
Centropyge flavissima - The lemon peel angelfish is one of the most underrated dwarf angelfish in the industry. Sporting some of the brightest yellow coloration with light blue accents around the eyes, gills, and sometimes lips this fish is sure to brighten up any aquarium. They stay relatively small with a 5” max sizing but tend to stay a bit smaller in home aquariums. We recommend using an acclimation box to introduce all new fish to the aquarium. They are cautiously reef safe if they are consistently well fed a varied diet and monitor any coral picking behavior closely. 

Please Choose a Friday Delivery

products.title: Sunrise Dottyback (Proaquatix) - Friday Delivery
products.body_html:
Pseudochromis flavivertex  - The Sunrise Dottyback is a beautiful blue Dottyback with a bright yellow stripe on the head and dorsal region. It's very hardy and easy to care for. While many dottybacks are aggressive, this species is more on the side of moderately aggressive. 

This species is valuable in reef tanks for its penchant for eating small bristleworms. Over time, they have been known to reduce numbers of pesky bristleworms in home aquariums. Dottybacks will eat most popular aquarium foods including a variety of frozen foods and Easy Reefs DKI pellets and Masstick. Feeding on C1, C2, Hikari Mysis, PE Calanus. 

Please Choose a Friday Delivery

products.title: Springeri Dottyback (Proaquatix) Friday Delivery
products.body_html:
Pseudochromis springeri - The Springeri Dottyback is a stunning fish with a black body and neon blue "racing stripes" on the face and head. This species is very hardy and easy to care for. 

Springeri Dottybacks are valuable in reef tanks for their penchant for eating small bristleworms. Over time, they have been known to reduce numbers of pesky bristleworms in home aquariums that are overrun. Dottybacks will eat most popular aquarium foods including a variety of frozen foods and Easy Reefs DKI pellets and Masstick. This Dottyback is semi-aggressive and requires a 30 gallon aquarium when full grown. 

Please choose a Friday Delivery

products.title: Yasha Goby Proaquatix (Friday Delivery)
products.body_html:
Stonogobiops yasha,  The Yasha Goby is one of the most striking and sought-after nano reef fish available, known for its crisp white body, bold red striping, and tall, elegant dorsal fin. This species naturally inhabits sandy reef flats where it forms a fascinating symbiotic relationship with pistol shrimp, sharing a burrow and communicating through constant tactile contact.

These Yasha Gobies are bred by Proaquatix and offered through The Biota Group, providing a hardier, aquarium-adapted alternative to wild collected specimens. Having been raised in captivity, they are well adjusted to aquarium life and readily accept a variety of prepared foods, including small pellets and frozen offerings. This makes them significantly easier to care for compared to their wild counterparts, which can be more delicate during acclimation.

In the home aquarium, the Yasha Goby does best in a well-established reef with a sandy substrate and plenty of rock structure for burrow placement. They can be kept singly or as a bonded pair, and they are completely reef safe, posing no threat to corals or invertebrates. When paired with a compatible pistol shrimp, their natural behavior becomes a captivating display, adding both visual interest and unique ecological interaction to the tank.

Yasha Gobies can be sexed when they reach adulthood/maturity. Females have solid clear-yellow pelvic fins, and males have black tips on the pelvic fins. At this young age, we're not able to guarantee the sex. Young Gobies have a more subdued coloration that will turn into the contrasting bright red and white as adults. 

Please choose a Friday delivery.

products.title: Biota Goldflake Green Chalice frag
products.body_html:
Echinophyllia sp. - This Goldflake Green Chalice has been growing in our Biota Florida facility for some time, and we are now offering in-house grown frags. 

The Orange Chalice prefers moderate water flow and lighting and will have the most intense coloration when it is slowly and carefully acclimated to brighter lighting, but also does well in the lower sections of a well-lit aquarium. Give it plenty of space to grow away from other corals. 

Like other LPS corals, this coral requires stable alkalinity, calcium, magnesium, and trace elements for health and skeleton building. It gets most of its nutrition from symbiotic zooxanthellae, but does best when fed a variety of coral foods and frozen foods.

products.title: Biota Palau Leptastrea Coral Ultra
products.body_html:
Leptastrea sp. - This ultra colored purple and neon green Leptastrea Coral is farmed in Palau. 

Leptastrea is a type of large polyp stony coral that will benefit from stable alkalinity, calcium, magnesium, and trace elements in the water. It prefers moderate water flow and light, and it does well in the lower sections of well-lit tanks. Use care when acclimating it to your lighting. 

This hardy species is a good choice for beginners who have a stable environment and water parameters. This coral will quickly encrust over any rock or adjacent surface.

products.title: Forest Fire Rhodactis Mushroom
products.body_html:
Forest Fire Rhodactis mushrooms are easy to keep and a great choice for beginners stocking a newly matured aquarium. These specimens are cultured and not taken from the reef. 

Mushroom corals need low to moderate lighting and water flow. Be sure to acclimate new coral to bright lighting slowly and carefully.

products.title: Blue Oxide Cardinalfish
products.body_html:
Ostorhinchus compressus - The Blue Oxide aka the Ochre Striped Cardinalfish or Blue Eyed Cardinalfish is a striking and underrated reef species that is finally becoming more accessible thanks to aquaculture. With its clean horizontal striping and active, midwater behavior, it offers a different look compared to more commonly kept cardinalfish. Biota’s success in captive breeding this species means aquarists can now enjoy a fish that has historically been scarce in the hobby, with all the added benefits of being aquarium-raised.

In the aquarium, O. compressus does best when kept in small groups or shoals. Unlike many marine fish that show aggression toward their own kind, these cardinals are naturally social and display more natural, confident behavior when housed together. A group of three can be kept in tanks around 50 gallons or larger, while bigger shoals really shine in longer aquariums, especially those 6 feet or more in length. Providing plenty of rockwork and hiding spaces may seem counterintuitive, but it actually helps them feel secure and encourages them to spend more time out in the open water column.

This species is generally considered reef safe, though caution is advised with very small fish or shrimp, as larger individuals, which can reach around 4.5 inches in captivity, may view them as food. Feeding is straightforward, as captive bred individuals readily accept a wide range of foods. A varied diet of frozen meaty foods along with prepared options like pellets and gels will keep them in excellent condition. With their peaceful nature, schooling behavior, and adaptability, Ostorhinchus compressus makes a unique and rewarding addition to a well-planned marine aquarium.

products.title: Palau Acropora/SPS Frag Pack
products.body_html:
Acropora/Seriatopora spp. - Four total aquacultured SPS frags included in the pack: 

1. Helen's Elegant Smoothskin turaki
2. Bubble's valida 
3. Thick green birdnest
4. Surprise "Assorted" Palau Acropora

This curated SPS pack brings together four aquacultured standouts from the genera Acropora and Seriatopora, offering a mix of texture, color, and growth forms that will elevate any reef. Each pack includes Helen’s Elegant Smoothskin turaki, Bubble’s valida, a thick green birdnest, and a surprise assorted Palau Acropora, giving you both named favorites and a unique wild card to enjoy as it grows and develops in your system. All frags are aquacultured, well-adapted to aquarium life, and selected for health and consistency.

For many reefkeepers, Acropora represent the pinnacle of the hobby. While they have a reputation for being more demanding, they reward stability and attention with incredible coloration and growth. These corals thrive under moderate to high lighting and strong, varied flow, and they benefit from a system that maintains stable alkalinity, calcium, magnesium, and trace elements like potassium. Consistency is key, once parameters are stable, these corals can truly take off.

All frags in this pack are grown under EcoTech Radion and Philips Coral Care lighting, ensuring they are well-acclimated to high-quality reef lighting. To support growth and coloration, we use and recommend Easy Reefs Easy Booster and Easy SPS coral food, which help provide the nutrients SPS corals need to thrive. Whether you’re building your first SPS-dominant reef or adding to an established system, this pack offers a strong foundation of proven performers.

products.title: T-Shirt — Tropical Yellow Fish Graphic Tee
products.body_html:
Soft, breathable cotton with a playful school of yellow-green fish drifting across the chest. This lightweight tee feels lived-in from the first wear and keeps its shape thanks to a ribbed crew neck and shoulder tape. The single-piece tubular knit gives a smooth silhouette with minimal seams, while tear-away labels and ethically sourced ring-spun cotton make it comfortable and responsible. Wear it on warm mornings by the water, at casual weekend markets, or layered under a jacket for a splash of color that reads like a small, personal adventure.

Product features
- 100% ring-spun cotton (lightweight 153 g/m²) with Oeko-Tex certification
- Tubular knit (no side seams) for a clean, smooth fit
- Ribbed crew neckline and shoulder tape for shape retention
- Pearlized tear-away label for added comfort; EU 2-year warranty
- DTF sleeve prints and DTG main prints; ethically sourced materials

Care instructions
- Do not dryclean
- Do not bleach
- Tumble dry: low heat
- Iron, steam or dry: low heat
- Machine wash: cold (max 30C or 90F), with similar colors

products.title: Trippy Mandarin Fish Swim Board Shorts — Vibrant Ocean Reef AOP
products.body_html:
These board shorts bring the reef to your rotation. Bright swirls of orange, teal and deep blue mimic coral and tropical fish, turning everyday beach time into a small, electric escape. Lightweight polyester moves with you and dries fast after a surf or quick dip. The elastic waistband and drawstring sit soft and secure whether you’re paddling out, strolling the boardwalk, or kicking back with friends. Deep side pockets keep your essentials handy without weighing you down. Wear them when you want color, movement, and a little ocean energy in your day.

Product features
- 100% polyester — durable, shape-retaining, and quick-drying
- Extra-light fabric (110 g/m²) for unrestricted movement
- Elastic waistband with black drawstring for a comfortable fit
- Side-seam pockets for secure, functional storage
- Vibrant all-over print with automatically matched seam thread

Care instructions
- Do not dryclean
- Iron, steam or dry: low heat
- Tumble dry: low heat
- Do not bleach
- Machine wash: cold (max 30C or 90F)

products.title: Annularis Angelfish
products.body_html:
Pomacanthus annularis - The Bluering Angelfish, also known as the Annularis Angelfish, is one of the true showstoppers of the marine aquarium world. As juveniles, they have a dark body marked with bright white and blue stripes, giving them a bold, high-contrast look that stands out immediately. As they mature, they undergo a dramatic transformation into striking adults with golden-orange bodies, electric blue accents, and the signature blue ring near the rear of the body that gives this species its common name. Watching that change take place over time is one of the most rewarding parts of keeping this fish.

This species as an adult has a big, confident presence and does best in a spacious aquarium with plenty of rockwork for grazing, exploration, and establishing territory. Bluering Angelfish are active swimmers and intelligent fish, quickly learning their environment and often becoming one of the most noticeable and interactive inhabitants in the tank. Their beauty and personality make them especially appealing for hobbyists looking for a centerpiece fish with both size and presence.

These juveniles should be kept in an acclimation box initially to help them acclimate to the new tank in safety. Avoid keeping small fish with large, aggressive fish.

Like many large angelfish, the Bluering/Annularis Angelfish is best suited for aquarists with some experience keeping marine fish. They appreciate a varied diet that includes angelfish preparations, algae-based foods, and other high-quality frozen or prepared options to support long-term health and coloration. In reef aquariums, caution is advised, as they may nip at corals, clam mantles, and other sessile invertebrates. In the right setup, though, this is a spectacular and rewarding species that brings movement, color, and unmistakable character to a large marine display.

products.title: False Personifer Angelfish
products.body_html:
Chaetodontoplus meredithi -  The Poma Labs False Personifer Angelfish, also known as Meredith's Angelfish, is a bold and impressive member of the Pomacanthidae family. Reaching up to 14 inches in length, this species is best suited for large, well established marine systems. Its dramatic coloration, black body with a blue green facial mask, yellow forehead, and bright yellow caudal fin accented by a blue green band. Males can be distinguished from females by their slightly longer body shape and speckled yellow facial pattern, a rare example of visible morphological differences between sexes in angelfish.

Juveniles should be introduced to an aquarium with an acclimation box or raised in a refugium or smaller aquarium before being introduced to larger or aggressive fish.

With a care level rated as moderate and a semi aggressive temperament, the False Personifer requires thoughtful planning when it comes to tank setup and tank mates. A minimum aquarium size of 180 gallons is recommended for an adult, with abundant live rock for grazing and territory, along with ample open swimming space.

When it comes to compatibility, this species is best housed as an adult with other semi aggressive to moderately assertive fish such as tangs, larger wrasses, and robust community species. Avoid keeping it with timid fish that may be bullied, or with other large angelfish unless the system is exceptionally spacious. Introducing tank mates carefully and providing defined territories helps reduce aggression.

Reef compatibility is considered “with caution.” The False Personifer is known to nip at both stony and soft corals, as well as clam mantles and other sessile invertebrates. For this reason, it is generally better suited to fish only systems or reefs stocked with hardy corals and managed with close observation.

As an omnivore, the False Personifer benefits from a varied and nutrient rich diet. Raised on small pellets and frozen foods, these captive bred fish are easier to keep than wild caught fish, Feed foods like Easy Reefs DKI 0.8mm pellets, Spirulina brine shrimp, maarine algae, and high quality angelfish preparations. A consistent, diverse feeding regimen supports immune health, vibrant coloration, and helps reduce the likelihood of coral nipping behavior. With proper care and space, this angelfish becomes a commanding and rewarding centerpiece in large marine aquariums.

These fish can not be delivered on Fridays. During checkout, please choose on the calendar a delivery date on a Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday only.

products.title: Neon Green Torch Coral
products.body_html:
Euphyllia - This Torch coral is a beautiful green color with purple tips, especially in actinic lighting. These are cultured corals not taken from the reef. 

Give plenty of space for this stinging coral to grow away from other species. Torch corals need moderate lighting and moderate water flow. It's necessary to maintain stable alkalinity, calcium, magnesium, and trace elements. Be sure to acclimate new coral to bright lighting slowly and carefully.

variants.title: Default Title
variants.sku: Euphyllia sp. (green w green tip torch)-R

products.title: Green Banded Goby
products.body_html:
Tigrigobius panamensis - The Green Banded Goby from Bocas Mariculture is a fantastic example of how small reef fish can have big personality. Captive bred and fully aquacultured, these gobies arrive hardy, well adapted to aquarium life, and already accustomed to prepared foods. Their crisp green and white banding gives them a clean, eye catching look that stands out even in larger tanks.

This species stays very small and is naturally peaceful and reef safe, making it an excellent choice for community reefs and smaller tanks, even pico tanks! Green Banded Gobies spend much of their time perched on rockwork or hovering just above the substrate, adding constant movement and charm without bothering tankmates or corals.

They can be kept successfully as a single fish, as a bonded pair, or even in small groups when provided with adequate space, rockwork, and hiding spots. In group settings, starting with multiple juveniles at the same time helps minimize territorial behavior and allows natural hierarchies to form.

They also mix well with other peaceful nano and pico gobies, including species from the genera Elacatinus, Trimma, and Eviota.

products.title: Rapunzel Coscinaraea
products.body_html:
Coscinaraea sp. This new release is an unmistakable oddball and a true conversation piece for LPS lovers.

Featuring smaller, tightly formed polyps paired with exceptionally long, flowing tentacles, this coral delivers movement and texture that immediately draws the eye. Its blue and green coloration comes alive under actinic lighting, producing an intense glow that makes it stand out even in well stocked reef systems.

Unlike more common large polyp LPS, this coral offers a more refined maze-like polyp structure while still providing dramatic extension and motion. The tentacles create a soft, flowing appearance as they sway in the current, making it especially striking in moderate, indirect flow.

This is not a typical, easily labeled coral, and that’s part of its appeal. It’s a unique addition for reef keepers who enjoy unusual morphology and rare finds, and it’s sure to spark discussion and curiosity in any display.

Lighting: Moderate to high, fluorescence peaks under actinics
Flow: Moderate, indirect to encourage full tentacle extension
Placement: Low to mid-level, with space for tentacle extension

An uncommon LPS with bold movement, strong fluorescence, and serious personality, perfect for reefers looking to add something truly different to their collection.

products.title: Lettuce Sea Slug
products.body_html:
Elysia sp. - The Lettuce Sea Slug is one of the most effective and fascinating natural tools for controlling nuisance hair algae in reef aquariums. With its flowing, lettuce leaf–like cerata and vibrant green, yellow, and brown coloration, it looks more like a living plant than an animal. These captive bred Lettuce Sea Slugs are already adapted to aquarium life and are significantly hardier than wild collected specimens.

Unlike true nudibranchs, Elysia crispata is a sacoglossan sea slug that actively feeds on problem filamentous green algae, including Bryopsis and Derbesia, two of the most frustrating algae pests reef keepers face. As it consumes these algae, it retains chloroplasts from its food through a process called kleptoplasty, allowing it to supplement its nutrition with light. This makes proper lighting and a steady algae supply important for long term success.

Lettuce Sea Slugs are completely reef safe and peaceful, and they will not bother corals, fish, or invertebrates. In mature systems with ongoing hair algae growth, they can play a meaningful role in reducing and managing nuisance algae naturally, making them both a functional and visually striking addition to the reef aquarium.

Important Acclimation Instructions

Please note: this product is exempt from our 7 day Livestock guarantee. It's normal for them to be shriveled up and lethargic on arrival. Check all the seams and lid, as slugs are small and difficult to see. After acclimating and feeding in your tank they will "perk up." These animals tolerate shipping very well, and it's exceedingly rare for them to die in shipping. Please contact us immediately and provide a short video clip if you have concerns about the slugs on arrival. Even if they appear to be DOA, please acclimate them, as it's normal for them to be lethargic during shipping. 

To acclimate slugs to your tank's temperature, float the closed cup or bag in the tank for 15 minutes. Turn off the flow and lights in your tank. Don't touch them with your fingers, and please handle the cup or bag gently since they're susceptible to physical damage. Ideally, you should slowly submerge then wedge the open shipping cup or bag into the rockwork in a very low flow area and allow them to crawl out naturally. If you absolutely must move a slug, use a turkey baster/coral feeder type instrument.

products.title: Captive Bred Narrow stripe Pipefish
products.body_html:
Doryrhamphus bicarinatus - Pipefish are close relatives of the seahorse. Narrow stripe pipefish are closely related to Bluestripe Pipefish and are among the most beautiful syngnathids with their bright blue and orange coloration. They are one of the "flagtail" pipefish and have relatively large, colorful caudal fins used for sailing around the tank in the current.

Wild caught pipefish are extremely difficult to keep because they typically only eat live foods. These captive bred pipefish from De Jong Marinelife are raised on prepared foods like frozen baby brine, chopped mysis, and PE Calanus, making them easier to keep. They're quite hardy when provided with the proper environment and care. Pipefish should be fed 3 - 4 x a day. 

Compatibility

Seahorse specific tanks are perfect environments for small pipefish. Unlike seahorses, Narrow stripe pipefish may also be suitable in a reef-style tank with proper tank mates. 

This pipefish species is small, and adults typically grow to 2" or 3" max. At sale size, young pipefish are about the size of a toothpick. Aggressive fish or fish large enough to eat small pipefish are not suitable tankmates. Beware larger crabs and hermit crabs or any animals that could pinch or harm them physically. Stinging anemones and any stinging corals like Euphyllia are risky tank mates for pipefish. Corals with large mouths can consume pipefish.  Many Doryrhamphus species are known to be active cleaners in the wild, picking tiny parasitic crustaceans from other fishes.

At sale size, these pipefish are too young to accurately sex. Adult male Narrow stripe Pipefish are very territorial with each other and will fight even in very large systems. This species is best kept singly or in pairs or harems with one male and multiple females. Adult males have tiny bumps on the snout that may only be seen with a magnifying glass. We recommend introducing new fish with an acclimation box.

Pipefish are very different from other aquarium fish, and their care and requirements are also unique to them. Feel free to reach out to us with any questions at support@thebiotagroup.com

products.title: Half Black Angelfish
products.body_html:
Vrolik’s Angelfish (Centropyge vrolikii) is a striking and often overlooked dwarf angelfish known for its dramatic contrast of deep charcoal coloration, electric blue striping, and bright orange accents. It offers all the personality and visual impact people love about Centropyge angelfish, while remaining a manageable size for many home aquariums.

These individuals are captive bred and raised in controlled conditions to ensure strong health, consistent feeding response, and adaptability to aquarium life. Aquacultured angelfish are typically hardier and better prepared for captivity than wild-caught specimens, making them an excellent choice for aquarists seeking long-term success.

Vrolik’s Angelfish does best in aquariums of 50 gallons or larger with plenty of live rock for grazing and shelter. It is considered semi-aggressive and, like most dwarf angelfish, is reef compatible with caution. Individual behavior can vary, and some may nip at certain corals. A varied omnivorous diet including Easy Reefs DKI pellets, frozen Spirulina brine, and frozen herbivore foods will help maintain health and coloration.

products.title: Breeder's Blend Frozen Food Biota x Rogger's Reef Foods
products.body_html:
Developed through a collaboration between The Biota Group and Rogger’s Reef Food, Breeder’s Blend is the ultimate frozen food for maintaining healthy, vibrant, and reproductively conditioned fish. This blend was originally created for use in Biota’s own breeding facilities, where optimal nutrition is essential for producing high-quality eggs and larvae.

Every ingredient in Breeder’s Blend has been chosen to support energy, immunity, and fertility. It’s made from wild-caught shrimp, squid, octopus, fresh clam and juice, mussels, and polychaete worms, along with fresh pressed garlic and 100% pure aloe vera for digestive and immune support. Enriched with copepods, fish eggs, and a complete vitamin profile, it provides critical sources of DHA, Omega-3, and Omega-6 fatty acids for enhanced health and coloration.

Proudly made in Florida, USA by Rogger’s Reef Food, Breeder’s Blend is the same premium formula we trust to nourish our own broodstock. Feed your fish the best and watch them thrive, display richer color, and breed successfully.

Keep frozen. Net weight: 7 oz.

Separate shipping charges may apply

products.title: Royal Gramma Basslet Proaquatix (Friday delivery)
products.body_html:
Gramma loreto - The Royal Gramma Basslet is a beloved reef fish known for its vibrant coloration, with a rich purple front that transitions into a bright golden yellow toward the tail. Its striking appearance and graceful swimming behavior make it a showpiece in both nano and larger reef aquariums.

In the wild, Royal Grammas inhabit coral reefs and rocky overhangs throughout the tropical western Atlantic, where they often shelter in crevices and caves. In the aquarium, they exhibit similar behavior, adding dynamic movement and personality to the midlevels of the tank while appreciating plenty of rockwork and hiding spots.

Proaquatix captive bred Royal Grammas readily accept high‑quality pelletized foods as well as frozen options such as ocean plankton, Mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, and other meaty fare. Generally peaceful and reef compatible, they make a fantastic centerpiece for a peaceful community aquarium.

Please Choose a Friday Delivery

products.title: Tiger Goby Proaquatix (Friday Delivery)
products.body_html:
Elacatinus puncticulatus - The Tiger Goby, is a small, striking goby with a translucent body patterned with bold brown vertical bars running from head to tail. Its subtle yet eye-catching color and dynamic presence make it a standout addition to nano and community reef aquariums. 

In the wild, these gobies are found along rockfaces, tidepools, and around large sponges throughout the subtropical western Atlantic, from Florida down through the Caribbean and northern South America. In the aquarium, they typically perch near crevices or rockwork, adding activity and interest to the lower levels of the tank. They can be kept as singles, in twos, in large groups, or with other species of nano gobies.

Captive-bred by Proaquatix, these gobies are hardy, disease-resistant, and trained to eat pellets, flakes, and frozen foods such as brine shrimp, Mysis, and chopped seafood. 

Please choose a Friday delivery.

products.title: Red Head Goby Proaquatix (Friday Delivery)
products.body_html:
Elacatinus puncticulatus - The Red Head Goby is a striking and ornate marine goby with a bright red head and delicate patterned body, highlighted by reflective yellow and blue stripes along its sides. Its translucent body and vibrant head make it an eye-catching addition to both nano and community reef aquariums. 

In nature this species lives among the spines of pencil urchins for protection, and in the aquarium it tends to perch near rockwork or crevices, adding activity and personality to the tank. These gobies are peaceful reef inhabitants and function as ideal scavengers, often helping to keep the environment clean and dynamic.  With their hardy nature, vivid coloration, and engaging behavior, they are well suited for nano or larger reef setups with peaceful tankmates. They can be kept singly, in twos, or in larger groups, or mixed with other nano goby species.

Captive-bred by Proaquatix, these gobies are hardy, disease-resistant, and trained to eat pellets, flakes, and frozen foods such as brine shrimp, Mysis, and chopped seafood. 

Please choose a Friday delivery.

products.title: Flametail Blenny Proaquatix (Friday Delivery)
products.body_html:
Enchelyurus flavipes - The Flametail Blenny, also known as the Yellowtail Black Blenny, features a sleek black body accented by bright yellow anal, caudal, and posterior dorsal fins. This energetic and personable blenny is often mistaken for the popular Bicolor Blenny, but it can be distinguished by its more rounded head and caudal fin, and by the absence of the small cirri between the eyes that are present on Ecsenius bicolor.

In the wild, adult Flametail Blennies inhabit shallow coral reefs in the Western Pacific, including the South China Sea. They are most often seen perching on rocks near the bottom, occasionally moving higher on the reef to graze on algal films and filamentous algae. This natural behavior makes them well suited for reef aquariums with established rockwork and available grazing surfaces.

The Flametail Blenny is generally peaceful and compatible with corals and most invertebrates, though it may show territorial behavior toward other blennies, small gobies, or dartfish. Proaquatix specimens are fully weaned onto a nutritionally complete pelletized diet and should also be offered marine algae, Spirulina, and frozen foods such as ocean plankton, Mysis shrimp, or brine shrimp for optimal health and coloration.

Captive-bred by Proaquatix, these blennies are hardy, disease-resistant, and trained to eat pellets, flakes, and frozen foods such as brine shrimp, Mysis, and chopped seafood. Their small adult size and calm demeanor make them a great choice for both nano and full-sized reef aquariums.

Please choose a Friday delivery.

products.title: Easy Reefs DKI Marine Pellet
products.body_html:
DKI Marine Pellet is the staple diet of fish here at the Biota facilities. If you're looking for the right pellets to feed your fish, you've come to the right page!

Why Freeze-dried?
Unlike traditional heat-processed foods that strip away vital nutrients, our freeze-drying
method locks in the raw, natural goodness of our ingredients. This means your fish receive a diet that is as close to their natural food source as possible, maximizing nutritional value and flavor.

The Foundation of a Complete Diet
A single microa lgae species, while nutritious, is often not enough to provide the complete balance diet that fish need to thrive. That is why we go beyond the standard and meticulously select a blend of four distinct microalgae:

• Nannochloropsis gaditana is our EPA powerhouse, delivering high concentrations of essential fatty acids boosting the immune system and overall physiological balance. • Tetraselmis chuii acts as our vitamin and amino acid source, providing a wide spectrum of essential vitamins and amino acids that support metabolism, reduce stress and promote
healthy growth. 
• Phaeodactylum tricornutum is key for color enhancement. It's rich in fucoxanthin, a powerful carotenoid pigment that naturally deepens and intensifies the vibrant colors of your fish, making them stand out. 
• lsochrysis galbana supplies a high level of DHA. This is vital for the development of the brain and nervous system development.

By combining these four powerful microalgae, we create a feed that is not only rich in nutrients but also diverse enough to meet all the dietary needs of your fish, providing a level of quality and completeness that single-species products simply cannot match 

DKI Marine
Aquatic Invertebrates:
A Purer Protein Alternative
• Rejecting conventional fish meal: Many feeds rely on fish meal, which is a processed by product of the fishing industry.
• The phosphate problem: Fish meal is often high in phosphates due to the inclusion of fish bones and other materials. Excess phosphates can lead to problems like nuisance algae blooms, which degrade water quality and require extra maintenance .
• our cleaner alternative: our feed uses naturally sourced aquatic invertebrates, which are freeze-dried to retain their full nutritional integrity and are inherently lower in phosphates.

A Natural and Palatable Diet - The use of whole freeze-dried aquatic invertebrates provides a high digestible, protein rich food source that mimics the natural diet of many fish, ensuring a strong feeding response and healthy growth, without the unwanted side effects of high phosphates. 

Specifications

Ingredients:
Aquatic invertebrates, glycerin, fish oil, macroalgae (Chondrus crispus and Ulva lactuta) and microalgae (lsochrysis galbana, Tetraselmis chuii, Phaeodactylum tricornuti.Jm and Na17nochloropsis gaditana)

Analytical constituents:
Protein (48.87%), Fat (11.20%), Ash (12.79%) and Fiber (5.60%). Energy: 1287 kJ / 307 kcal/100 g. 

Can ship in your fish order! When ordering only dry goods, you can choose UPS Ground. To guarantee calendar delivery date, choose Next Day Air delivery.

products.title: Net Sea Fan Panama
products.body_html:
Gorgonia sp. (likely Gorgonia mariae) This variety of Gorgonian, the Net Sea Fan, was cultured in Panama at Bocas Mariculture. It has thin, purple, elegant branches with small polyps that fuse together as they grow to form an intricate net pattern. This type of sea fan, including Gorgonia ventalina and G. flabellum, has been sought after but difficult for hobbyists to obtain in years past, but is now available through Biota as an aquacultured alternative to wild collection. 

Many other species of gorgonians and sea fans are difficult to keep in captivity, however, this species is relatively easy to keep and a great coral for beginners who want something with interesting form and flow. It is photosynthetic, but will benefit from feedings of microfoods designed for soft corals.

products.title: Yucatan Shrimp with Rose Bubble Tip Anemone
products.body_html:
Periciminels yucitanicus & Entacmea quadricolor - The Yucatan Shrimp and Mini Bubble Tip Anemone combo is a unique and captivating symbiotic pair that brings personality and movement to nano and pico reef aquariums. The Yucatan Shrimp, aquacultured by Bocas Mariculture, forms a natural bond with the miniature anemone and spends its time sheltered among the tentacles, creating a dynamic focal point for your tank.

The Rose Bubble Tip Anemone in this combo is an aquacultured variety from Biota Florida. The Yucatan Shrimp has a clear body with beautiful blue and white patterning. It is an active shrimp, often seen cleaning itself or waving its antennae from within the anemone's tentacles.

Both animals are aquacultured in controlled environments, which makes them hardy, adaptable, and ready for life in your tank.  The Yucatan Shrimp and Mini Bubble Tip Anemone combo is perfect for hobbyists looking for something different, eye catching, and full of personality. It is a sustainable and exciting addition to any nano reef.

variants.title: Default Title
variants.sku: Periclimines yucatanicus & Entacmaea quadricolor(Yucatan shrimp & Bubble tip anemone)-R

products.title: Biota Galaxea frag
products.body_html:
Galaxea sp - The Biota Captive Bred Encrusting Galaxea is a vibrant, fast growing coral that spreads across rockwork with glowing polyps and a rich, textured base. This aquacultured strain has been raised in controlled conditions from the start, making it an excellent choice for reef keepers who want long term success without the risks often associated with wild collected corals.

Galaxea is a type of large polyp stony coral that will benefit from stable alkalinity, calcium, magnesium, and trace elements in the water. It prefers moderate water flow and light, and it does well in the lower sections of well-lit tanks. Use care when acclimating it to your lighting. 

This hardy species is a good choice for beginners who have a stable environment and water parameters. This coral will quickly grow new branches.

products.title: Cosmic Toadstool
products.body_html:
The Cosmic Toadstool coral from Biota is a special variety with flower or star-like polyps with green centers and white tentacle tips. 

Toadstool corals are fast growing and hardy, easy to keep soft corals and a great choice for beginners stocking a newly matured reef aquarium. These specimens were cultured and not taken from the reef.

Toadstool corals need moderate to high lighting and water flow. Be sure to acclimate new coral to bright lighting slowly and carefully.

products.title: cultured Green Hammer frag
products.body_html:
Euphyllia - This variety of green hammer coral is cultured and not taken from the reef. 

Give this stinging species plenty of space to grow away from other corals. 

Hammer corals need moderate lighting and water flow. It's necessary to maintain stable alkalinity, calcium, magnesium, and trace elements. Be sure to acclimate new coral to bright lighting slowly and carefully.

products.title: Klein's Butterflyfish
products.body_html:
Chaetodon kleinii - Biota captive bred Klein’s Butterflyfish is a hardy, active, and stunning species now available captive bred from Biota. These graceful fish feature shimmering yellow bodies with soft brown bands and a subtle blue tint on their fins, making them a beautiful and peaceful addition to most community aquariums.

Biota’s Klein’s Butterflyfish are 100% captive bred and raised, which means they’re already fully acclimated to aquarium life. Unlike wild caught butterflies, our fish are raised on prepared foods like Easy Reefs DKI pellets. Offer a variety of meaty foods and algae based diets to keep them in top condition.

Klein’s Butterflyfish are known for being one of the most adaptable and peaceful butterfly species. They do well in community tanks with other non aggressive fish. While they may occasionally pick at coral polyps or some soft corals, many reefkeepers successfully keep them in mixed reef tanks with proper feeding and observation.

products.title: Cultured Elegance Coral
products.body_html:
Catalaphyllia jardenei - Bring the grace and color of the reef to your aquarium with Biota cultured Elegance Corals. These stunning LPS corals are known for their flowing tentacles, vibrant shades of green, purple, and pink, and their unmistakable “elegant” appearance that makes them a centerpiece in any reef tank.

Unlike wild Elegance corals, which are often short-lived in captivity and prone to disease, our corals are carefully propagated from healthy parent colonies. Fragments are cut, then grown out for up to a year in pristine aquaculture systems until they are fully healed, robust, and ready for home aquariums. This process produces corals that are hardier, more adaptable, and far more sustainable than wild-collected specimens.

Why Cultured Elegance Corals Are Better

Wild Elegance corals can be unpredictable. Many develop tissue infections shortly after import, leading to poor survival rates. Cultured Elegance corals, however, are fully acclimated to aquarium conditions and thrive under proper care.

Care and Placement

Elegance corals prefer moderate lighting and gentle to moderate flow that allows their tentacles to sway naturally without being blasted. Place them on the sand bed or a stable, low rock ledge where their fleshy base can expand comfortably. They appreciate stable water parameters and occasional target feeding with meaty foods such as mysis shrimp or coral pellets like Easy Reefs Easy LPS.

Quick Facts

  • Lighting: Moderate (PAR 80–150)

  • Flow: Gentle to moderate

  • Placement: Sand bed or low rock ledge

  • Temperament: Semi-aggressive; give space from neighbors

  • Feeding: Accepts fine meaty foods and coral feeds

Coloration:

Elegance corals show a wide range of coloration, with several commonly recognized patterns.

Sprung Elegance:  Neon green center with green tentacles and pink or purple tips. Some may also show purple streaking in the center.

Green:  The classic form, with varying shades of green across the center and tentacles, typically ending in pink or purple tips. Purple streaking in the center can also occur.

Neon Green: A more vibrant variation featuring a bright neon green center and matching neon or green tentacles, with pink or purple tips. Some may also display purple streaking in the center.

Purple:  Purple center and tentacles, usually paired with pink or purple tips

Cultured Elegance Corals combine beauty, resilience, and sustainability. When you choose a Biota-grown Elegance, you’re not only getting a coral with the best chance to thrive, you’re also supporting the future of responsible reefkeeping.

products.title: Long Arm Shrimp
products.body_html:
Cuapetes sp. - The Long Arm Shrimp is a small and elegant shrimp species found throughout the tropical Indo-Pacific, including Palau. Shrimp in this genus are often seen living in close association with corals, anemones, or crinoids, where their transparent bodies and subtle striping help them blend perfectly into their surroundings. This camouflage makes them almost invisible to predators and an incredible example of reef adaptation.

In the wild, Cuapetes act as gentle scavengers, feeding on small bits of detritus, leftover food, and organic material around their hosts. They don't require a host for survival in aquariums and will eat just about any aquarium foods. They are peaceful and reef safe, making them great additions to nano and pico aquariums with other tiny, non-aggressive tank mates like nano gobies and shrimp like Periclimenes and Urocaridella spp.

These shrimp prefer an environment with plenty of structure, such as branching coral, sponge, or macroalgae, where they can perch and explore. Do not house them with fish that are large enough to eat them. 

Current size: 0.25" - 0.5"

products.title: Amboina Cardinalfish
products.body_html:
Fibramia amboinensis - The Ambon or Amboina Cardinalfish, is a reef and mangrove-associated cardinalfish that has recently become available as captive bred from Biota, making it a rare and exciting addition for aquarists.

Ambon Cardinalfish are social and thrive in small groups or shoals. A trio can comfortably live in a tank 50 gallons or larger, while larger groups do best in long, spacious aquariums. Providing plenty of caves, overhangs, and secure hiding spots encourages natural behaviors and helps the fish feel safe, while still allowing them to be active and visible in the water column.

They are reef safe and peaceful, though very small fish and invertebrates may be at risk from adult Ambon Cardinalfish, which generally reach around 4 inches in captivity. We recommend a varied diet of frozen meaty foods and Easy Reefs DKI pellets and Masstick.

Captive bred Fibramia amboinensis are an ideal choice for aquarists seeking a hardy, social, and visually engaging cardinalfish for their reef setup.

products.title: Reese's Clownfish Proaquatix (Friday Delivery)
products.body_html:
Amphiprion ocellaris - Reeses are Mocha Ocellaris that have been selectively bred to exhibit no barring/stripes. The resulting fish is completely mocha or caramel in color giving a peanut butter and chocolate color which is how it was given the name Reeses.

The A. ocellaris clownfish is peaceful by clownfish standards and a great choice for both beginner and experienced aquarists. It thrives in reef tanks and does well in pairs or small groups when introduced properly. This variety is tank-bred by Proaquatix in the USA and has been raised on prepared foods, making it well-adjusted to aquarium life.

Clownfish exhibit a clear social hierarchy. In a group or pair, the largest fish becomes the female while the next largest becomes male. This species is reef safe and compatible with most other reef safe fish. Their natural host anemones are H. magnifica, S. gigantea, and S. mertensii, but they often adopt Bubble Tip Anemones, rockwork, coral, or algae as substitutes for host anemones.

products.title: Green Pipe Organ frag
products.body_html:
Tubipora musica: Add a truly unique centerpiece to your reef with our cultured Pipe Organ Coral frag. This coral gets its name from its striking skeletal structure that resembles the pipes of a church organ, topped with vibrant polyps that sway beautifully in the current.

Our Pipe Organ frags are sustainably cultured, ensuring you receive a healthy, aquarium-acclimated specimen that thrives in home reef systems. Unlike wild-harvested colonies, these aquacultured frags are hardier and better adapted to aquarium conditions, making them an excellent choice for reef keepers of all levels.

Pipe Organs are relatively fast growers under the right conditions and can form impressive colonies over time. They prefer moderate light and flow, which allows their delicate polyps to extend and wave gently, adding motion and texture to your aquascape. 

Although Tubipora musica is classified as a soft coral, it stands out because it develops a hard, organ pipe-like skeleton made of fused calcium carbonate tubes. This skeletal structure is what gives the coral its distinct appearance and supports the soft, fleshy polyps that emerge from each tube. The combination of a rigid framework and delicate polyps makes Pipe Organ corals both visually unique and scientifically interesting, bridging the characteristics of soft corals and stony corals in one species.

products.title: Biota Green Favia frag
products.body_html:
Faviid: These "Favia" frags are aquacultured by Biota. These are a type of large polyp stony coral that will benefit from stable alkalinity, calcium, magnesium, and trace elements in the water. It prefers moderate water flow and light, and it does well in the lower sections of well-lit tanks. Use care when acclimating it to your lighting. 

This hardy species is a good choice for beginners who have a stable environment and water parameters. This coral will quickly encrust over any rock or adjacent surface.

products.title: Biota Blue Stylophora frag
products.body_html:
Stylophora - This SPS coral is hardier and easier to keep than the similar Acropora coral, but has similar elegant branching shape and multiple beautiful colors. These frags are cultured and not taken from the reef.

Stylophora need moderate to high lighting and moderate to high water flow. It's necessary to maintain stable alkalinity, calcium, magnesium, and trace elements. Be sure to acclimate new coral to bright lighting slowly and carefully.

products.title: Red Blastomussa Colony
products.body_html:
Blastomussa wellsi. - Bring bold red color to your reef with our cultured Red Blastomussa colony. This LPS coral is known for its rich crimson tones and fleshy, polyp-like structure that gives a striking pop against live rock and other corals. Grown sustainably, these colonies are hardy and well-adapted to aquarium life, making them an excellent choice for both new and experienced reef keepers.

Blastomussa thrive in moderate lighting and gentle to moderate flow, where their fleshy heads can expand fully. They’re peaceful corals that won’t bother most neighbors. This large polyp stony coral that will benefit from stable alkalinity, calcium, magnesium, and trace elements in the water. It does well in the lower sections of well-lit tanks. Use care when acclimating it to your lighting. 

Every colony is cultured, so you can enjoy vibrant, healthy coral while supporting sustainable reefkeeping practices.

products.body_html:

This classic "Favia" is a type of large polyp stony coral that will benefit from stable alkalinity, calcium, magnesium, and trace elements in the water. It prefers moderate water flow and light, and it does well in the lower sections of well-lit tanks. Use care when acclimating it to your lighting. 

This hardy species is a good choice for beginners who have a stable environment and water parameters. This coral will quickly encrust over any rock or adjacent surface.

products.title: The Right Pellets For My Fish
products.body_html:
Not sure which food is best for your new fish? Let us take the guesswork out of it. Just add “The Right Pellets for My Fish” to your order, and we’ll include the pellet food your fish is already eating here at Biota.

That means your new fish will recognize the food right away, making feeding easier for you and less stressful for them.

✔️ Perfect for new aquarists or anyone ordering multiple species
✔️ We hand-select the correct pellet food based on the fish in your order
✔️ Ensures your fish continues eating the diet it’s trained on here

Leave it to us, and your fish will thank you.

FAQs

What if I order more than one fish?
No problem! We’ll include a pellet that is suitable for all the species on your order. The only exceptions are special feeders like seahorses or other “expert care” fish.

What size are the pellets?
Pellet size varies depending on the fish species, so we’ll make sure you get the right size for your fish.

Can I reorder the same food later?
Yes! Once you know what your fish are eating, you can find and reorder the same food directly from our store.

products.title: Flurry Clownfish Proaquatix (Friday Delivery)
products.body_html:
Amphiprion ocellaris - Flurry clownfish are the resultant cross between Wyoming Whites and Extreme Snowflakes. The resulting fish takes after the Wyoming White side of the physical traits with it having a fully white body. However, it has a white face mask, and strong black coloring on all the edges of the fish and all its fins are black, as well as the white being more jagged like the edges of a snowflake, unlike the Wyoming White where all their edges are smooth. Sometimes their white color gives off a bluish hue which gives it the name sapphire.  
The A. ocellaris clownfish is peaceful by clownfish standards and a great choice for both beginner and experienced aquarists. It thrives in reef tanks and does well in pairs or small groups when introduced properly. This variety is tank-bred by Proaquatix in the USA and has been raised on prepared foods, making it well-adjusted to aquarium life.

Clownfish exhibit a clear social hierarchy. In a group or pair, the largest fish becomes the female while the next largest becomes male. This species is reef safe and compatible with most other reef safe fish. Their natural host anemones are H. magnifica, S. gigantea, and S. mertensii, but they often adopt Bubble Tip Anemones, rockwork, coral, or algae as substitutes for host anemones.

Please choose a Friday delivery

Black margins are found on all the edges of every fin, however they are jagged lines like that of the snowflakes. They have an orange face mask, and some orange coloring can be found on the pectoral fins and possibly on others. What sets them apart is there are black dots on one or both sides of the fish with a total of 10 dots or more.

products.title: Biota Pink & Blue Chalice frag
products.body_html:
Echinophyllia sp. - This stunning chalice coral is red with blue-green streaks. It has been growing in our Biota Florida facility for some time, and we are now offering in-house grown frags. 

The Red & Blue Chalice prefers moderate water flow and lighting and will have the most intense coloration when it is slowly and carefully acclimated to brighter lighting, but also does well in the lower sections of a well-lit aquarium. Give it plenty of space to grow away from other corals. 

Like other LPS corals, this coral requires stable alkalinity, calcium, magnesium, and trace elements for health and skeleton building. It gets most of its nutrition from symbiotic zooxanthellae, but does best when fed a variety of coral foods and frozen foods.

products.title: Mozambique Fangblenny
products.body_html:
Meiacanthus mossambicus - The Mozambique Fangblenny, also known as the Harptail Blenny, is an outgoing, curious species of fang blenny. With its elongated body and bold horizontal stripes of blue and black, this species brings both color and personality to the tank. Most blennies are shy and hide most of the time, but not the Harptail Blenny. It's extra gregarious because it doesn't have much to worry about due to its secret weapon - venomous fangs. It's extremely rare for aquarists to receive a bite; just be careful not to accidentally squeeze or harass your blenny. In the wild if a fish tries to gobble them, they inflict a painful bite, and the fish (hopefully) releases them. This confidence allows them to swim out in the open, interacting with and entertaining their humans.

This is an incredibly hardy and reef safe species with beautiful coloration and lots of personality. This listing is for a single Fangblenny, but they do well in small tanks of 30 gallons or more singly, in pairs, or in small groups in larger aquariums. We recommend a variety of foods like frozen mysis, Easy Reefs DKI pellets and Masstick, C1 and C2 pellets, PE Calanus.

products.title: Black Torch with Green Tips
products.body_html:
Euphyllia glabrescens- This Biota Green Tip Black Torch frag is sought after for its interesting coloration and flowing tentacles. These come from cultured corals not taken from the reef. 

Give plenty of space for this stinging coral to grow away from other species. Torch corals need moderate lighting and moderate water flow. It's necessary to maintain stable alkalinity, calcium, magnesium, and trace elements. Be sure to acclimate new coral to bright lighting slowly and carefully.

products.title: Ocellaris Clownfish Proaquatix (Friday Delivery)
products.body_html:
Amphiprion ocellaris - The Orange Ocellaris Clownfish is a classic favorite among reef aquarists, known for its bright orange coloration with crisp white bands and friendly demeanor. Bred by Proaquatix in the USA, these clownfish are aquacultured to thrive in home aquariums. They are hardy, peaceful, and an excellent choice for both beginner and experienced reefkeepers.

This species naturally hosts in a variety of anemones in the wild, most commonly the Heteractis magnifica (Magnificent Sea Anemone) and Stichodactyla gigantea (Gigantic Carpet Anemone), though in aquariums they can adapt to a range of suitable host anemones or even live comfortably without one.

Aquacultured clownfish like these are a sustainable alternative to wild-caught specimens. They arrive well-acclimated to aquarium life, readily accept prepared foods, and help reduce pressure on wild populations. Ocellaris Clownfish also do well in pairs or small groups and can be a charming and active addition to community tanks.

When combining Proaquatix with Biota fish in the same order, please choose a Friday delivery

products.title: Longnose Butterflyfish
products.body_html:
Forcipiger flavissimus - Biota is proud to introduce a world’s first: the commercially available captive bred Longnose Butterflyfish. This striking species is now available thanks to a groundbreaking aquaculture collaboration between Biota Hawaii and the Oceanic Institute at Hawaii Pacific University.

Longnose Butterflyfish are known for their vivid yellow bodies, elongated snouts, and peaceful personalities. Until now, these fish were only available as wild-caught, often with a reputation for being delicate and challenging in captivity. Our captive bred individuals are adapted to aquarium life and eating prepared foods like DKI pellets and frozen foods, making them hardier and far more sustainable choices for reef aquarists.

This species is considered to be "reef safe with caution" and may pick at corals and sessile inverts. Provide ample rockwork for hiding and grazing. Like most butterflyfish, they prefer mature aquariums with stable conditions.

products.title: Striped Blenny Proaquatix (Friday Delivery)
products.body_html:
Meiacanthus grammistes - The Striped Fang Blenny is easily recognized by its bold horizontal black and white stripes, which gradually fade into a yellow wash toward the head. Its streamlined body and curious nature make it a standout addition to reef tanks, where it often hovers mid-water near rock crevices or darts in and out of hiding spots.

Native to a wide range across the Pacific, from Southern Japan to the Great Barrier Reef, this species plays a unique role in the reef ecosystem. Like others in its genus, M. grammistes has a mild venomous bite, used strictly for defense. This natural defense mechanism, along with its vibrant warning coloration, protects it from predators. It's so effective that several harmless fish species mimic this blenny’s look to avoid being eaten.

Despite its built-in defenses, this is a peaceful, reef-safe fish that typically ignores both corals and other fish species. It’s well suited to community reef tanks and does especially well in tanks with plenty of live rock and open swimming space.

Captive-bred by Proaquatix, these blennies are hardy, disease-resistant, and trained to eat pellets, flakes, and frozen foods such as brine shrimp, Mysis, and chopped seafood. Their small adult size and calm demeanor make them a great choice for both nano and full-sized reef aquariums.

Please choose a Friday delivery.

products.title: Neon Dottyback Proaquatix (Friday Delivery)
products.body_html:
Pseudochromis aldabrensis - With its bright orange body and iridescent blue stripes along the dorsal and head, the Neon Dottyback brings both color and attitude to any reef aquarium. Two bold blue lines run the length of the body and fins, with additional short markings across the face, giving this fish a truly striking appearance.

Native to the waters off the eastern coast of Africa and as far south as South Africa, this species is a natural predator of small invertebrates and worms, and will spend most of its time darting among the rocks and crevices of the reef. Like other dottybacks, it is territorial, especially toward its own kind and similarly shaped fish, and does best either singly or as a known bonded pair.

Captive-bred by Proaquatix, these fish are hardy, reef safe, and well-adjusted to aquarium life. They have been raised on pellets and flakes, and eagerly take frozen foods like Mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, and chopped seafood.

While they may nip at very small ornamental shrimp, they usually leave corals and most other inverts alone. They are also known for helping reduce unwanted pests like bristle worms, making them a great addition to a pest-control-minded reef keeper’s system.

Please choose a Friday delivery.

products.title: Orchid Dottyback Proaquatix (Friday Delivery)
products.body_html:
Pseudochromis fridmani - The Orchid Dottyback is a striking reef fish known for its brilliant, electric purple body and contrasting black eye stripe. Native to the Red Sea, this species has become a favorite among reef aquarists for its bold color and manageable temperament.

Unlike many other dottybacks, P. fridmani is notably more peaceful, making it one of the more community-friendly choices within its genus. While it may chase very small shrimp in tight spaces, it usually leaves other fish, corals, and invertebrates alone. It may fight with conspecifics or similar looking species. In fact, it can be a useful addition for helping control populations of unwanted pests like bristle worms.

Captive-bred by Proaquatix, these dottybacks are weaned onto pellets and flakes, and readily accept frozen foods such as Mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, and chopped seafood. They are hardy, reef safe, and well-suited to both small and larger aquariums with adequate rockwork and hiding spots.

When combining Proaquatix with Biota fish in the same order, please choose a Friday delivery.

products.title: Yellow Neon Cleaner Goby Proaquatix (Friday Delivery)
products.body_html:
Elacatinus figaro - The Yellow Neon Goby stands out with a vibrant, reflective yellow stripe that runs the length of its sleek black and white body. Small in size but big in personality, this species is a popular choice for reef aquariums thanks to its color, peaceful nature, and helpful behavior.

In the wild, E. figaro lives in groups along the Brazilian coast, where it establishes cleaning stations. Larger fish will actively seek them out, pausing to have parasites and dead skin gently picked from their gills, mouths, and fins. This cleaning instinct often continues in the home aquarium, adding a fascinating and beneficial dynamic to your tank.

These captive-bred specimens from Proaquatix are fully weaned onto prepared foods like pellets and flakes, and also enjoy frozen options such as Mysis shrimp and brine. Unlike some wild cleaner species, they thrive in captivity and readily accept standard aquarium diets.

Peaceful and reef safe, Yellow Neon Gobies can be kept singly or in groups, and they do well in both nano tanks and larger reef systems. They are compatible with most other peaceful fish, though like other small gobies, they should not be housed with aggressive or ambush predators.

Please choose a Friday delivery.

products.title: Blue Neon Cleaner Goby Proaquatix (Friday Delivery)
products.body_html:
Elacatinus oceanops - The Blue Neon Goby is small with a bold iridescent blue stripe that runs from nose to tail, set against a sleek black-and-white body. Native to the waters of Florida and the Caribbean, this tiny goby adds vibrant color and utility to any reef tank.

This species is well known for its natural cleaning behavior. In the wild, Blue Neon Gobies set up cleaning stations where larger fish voluntarily stop to have parasites and dead skin removed. That behavior often continues in the aquarium, making them not just beautiful but functional tankmates. They’ll gently pick at debris on other fish while also readily accepting prepared foods like pellets, flakes, and frozen fare.

Our captive-bred specimens from Proaquatix are hardy and reef safe. They are peaceful with most other species and can be kept singly or in small groups in tanks with plenty of rockwork and swimming space. At around 2 inches fully grown, they’re perfect for both nano and larger reef aquariums.

When combining Proaquatix with Biota fish in the same order, please choose a Friday delivery.

products.title: Sharknose Goby Proaquatix (Friday Delivery)
products.body_html:
Elacatinus evelynae - With its electric blue and yellow racing stripes and sleek, streamlined body, the Sharknose Goby is more than just eye-catching, it’s also a functional member of your reef tank community. This Caribbean native is known for setting up cleaning stations, where it picks parasites and dead tissue off larger fish. It's a natural symbiotic behavior that adds both interest and utility to your aquarium.

This variety is captive bred in the USA by Proaquatix, raised entirely on prepared foods, and well-adjusted to aquarium life. That means it's hardier, more adaptable, and a sustainable alternative to wild-caught gobies.

Sharknose Gobies are peaceful and reef safe, making them a great choice for nano tanks, mixed reef systems, and even peaceful fish-only aquariums. They do well singly, in pairs, or in groups if given enough space. Watching them hover in the water column or interact with tankmates at their "cleaning station" is just one of the many reasons they’re such a popular choice.

When combining Proaquatix with Biota fish in the same order, please choose a Friday delivery.

products.title: Yellow Assessor Proaquatix (Friday Delivery)
products.body_html:
Assessor flavissimus - A brilliant yellow reef fish with a compact body and striking coloration. The Yellow Assessor is known for its unusual behavior of swimming upside down under ledges and overhangs, adding a unique touch to the aquarium. Its vivid yellow color makes it a sought after fish for peaceful nano and reef tanks.

The Yellow Assessor is peaceful and reef safe. It thrives in aquariums with plenty of rockwork and shaded areas where it can perch or hover. This species is captive bred in the USA by Proaquatix and has been raised on prepared foods, making it hardy and well-adjusted to aquarium life.

This species is reef safe and can be kept singly, in pairs, or potentially in small groups in larger tanks with ample territory. Assessors are born female, and the dominant one in a group or pair becomes male as they mature. It is compatible with most other peaceful reef fish and avoids open water, preferring to hover near structure or retreat into caves when startled.

Please choose a Friday delivery.

products.title: Captive Bred Janss' Pipefish
products.body_html:
Doryrhamphus janssi - Pipefish are close relatives of the seahorse. Janss' pipefish are among the most beautiful syngnathids with their bright blue and orange coloration. They are one of the "flagtail" pipefish and have relatively large caudal fins used for sailing around the tank in the current.

Wild caught pipefish are extremely difficult to keep because they typically only eat live foods. These captive bred pipefish from David Sowash at Reef Life Creations are raised on prepared foods like frozen baby brine, chopped mysis, and PE Calanus, making them easier to keep. They're quite hardy when provided with the proper environment and care. Pipefish should be fed 3 - 5 x a day. 

Compatibility

Seahorse specific tanks are perfect environments for pipefish. Unlike seahorses, Janss' pipefish may also be suitable in a reef-style tank with proper tank mates. Many Doryrhamphus species like Janss' are known to be active cleaners in the wild, picking tiny parasitic crustaceans from other fishes.

Aggressive fish or fish large enough to eat small pipefish are not suitable tankmates. Beware larger crabs and hermit crabs or any animals that could pinch or harm them physically. Stinging anemones and any stinging corals like Euphyllia are risky tank mates for pipefish. Corals with large mouths can consume pipefish.  We recommend introducing new fish with an acclimation box.

Pipefish are very different from other aquarium fish, and their care and requirements are also unique to them. Feel free to reach out to us with any questions at support@thebiotagroup.com

products.title: Twin Spot Goby
products.body_html:
Signigobius biocellatus - The Biota Palau Captive-Bred Twin Spot Goby, also known as the Twin Spot or Two Spot Signal Goby, is a striking and unique sand-sifting species prized for its bold pattern and peaceful behavior. Recognized by the two distinct eyespots on its dorsal fins and its fascinating scooting movement across the sandbed, this goby adds both function and beauty to a reef or marine aquarium.

Unlike wild-caught Twin Spot Gobies, which are notoriously difficult to acclimate and often struggle to thrive in captivity, Biota’s captive-bred specimens are conditioned to accept prepared diets such as high-quality Easy Reefs DKI pellets and frozen foods. As a result, they are much hardier, less stressed, and far more adaptable to home aquarium conditions than their wild counterparts.

These captive-bred Twin Spot Gobies make excellent additions to peaceful community tanks, particularly reef aquariums with soft sand substrates where they can exhibit their natural sifting behavior. These gobies are small enough to fit in nano tanks, and interesting enough to go in a larger reef tank.  We strongly recommend introducing these small fish using an acclimation box . 

Their diet should include We recommend Easy Reefs DKI pellets and Masstick plus a variety of small frozen foods like Calanus and baby brine shrimp to ensure proper nutrition and long-term health.

With their charming appearance, peaceful nature, and captive-bred resilience, Biota’s Twin Spot Gobies are a responsible and rewarding choice for aquarists who want something truly special for their reef tank.

The A. ocellaris clownfish is peaceful by clownfish standards and a great choice for both beginner and experienced aquarists. It thrives in reef tanks and does well in pairs or small groups when introduced properly. This variety is tank-bred by Proaquatix in the USA and has been raised on prepared foods, making it well-adjusted to aquarium life.

Clownfish exhibit a clear social hierarchy. In a group or pair, the largest fish becomes the female while the next largest becomes male. This species is reef safe and compatible with most other reef safe fish. Their natural host anemones are H. magnifica, S. gigantea, and S. mertensii, but they often adopt Bubble Tip Anemones, rockwork, coral, or algae as substitutes for host anemones.

Like other Ocellaris, the Black Ice is peaceful by clownfish standards and a great choice for both beginner and experienced aquarists. It thrives in reef tanks and does well in pairs or small groups when introduced properly. This variety is tank-bred by Proaquatix in the USA and has been raised on prepared foods, making it well-adjusted to aquarium life.

Black Ice Clownfish exhibit a clear social hierarchy. In a group or pair, the largest fish becomes the female while the next largest becomes male. This species is reef safe and compatible with most other reef safe fish. Their natural host anemones are H. magnifica, S. gigantea, and S. mertensii, but they often adopt Bubble Tip Anemones, rockwork, coral, or algae as substitutes for host anemones.
